task_completion.description: Configure two turbines at positions (0, 0, 0) and (50, 0, 0)
task_completion.visual_evidence: QBlade multi-turbine setup or LLT simulation configuration window showing two turbines with coordinates X=0, Y=0, Z=0 and X=50, Y=0, Z=0.

task_completion.description: Set simulation parameters: 12 m/s wind speed, 0.1 s timestep, 200 timesteps
task_completion.visual_evidence: QBlade LLT simulation setup panel showing 'Wind Speed' = 12, 'Timestep' = 0.1, and 'Number of Timesteps' (or similar) = 200.

task_completion.description: Execute the LLT simulation to analyze wake shadowing
task_completion.visual_evidence: QBlade 3D view showing vortex wake lines behind the turbines, or a results graph/table displaying power outputs for both turbines.
QBlade window title showing 'wake_study.wpa', or a file manager/terminal showing the file 'wake_study.wpa' in /home/ga/Documents/projects/.

task_completion.description: Create the wake loss report with the correct format
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal showing the contents of /home/ga/Documents/wake_loss_report.txt with exactly three lines: 'Upstream Power: [Value] kW', 'Downstream Power: [Value] kW', and 'Wake Loss: [Value] %'.

integrity.description: Agent used the QBlade GUI to configure and run the simulation
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the QBlade application open and being interacted with, rather than just terminal commands writing a fake .wpa file.

integrity.description: Report values are derived from actual simulation results
integrity.visual_evidence: The power values in the text report match or closely correspond to the power output graphs or tables visible in the QBlade simulation results.

integrity.description: Wake loss is physically realistic and not arbitrarily fabricated
integrity.visual_evidence: The downstream power is strictly less than the upstream power, and the calculated wake loss percentage is mathematically correct based on the two power values (expected to be >= 10%).

privileged_info_for_vlm: QBlade v0.96.2 uses Lifting Line Theory (LLT) with a Free Vortex Wake model. The task requires setting up two turbines at (0,0,0) and (50,0,0), with 12 m/s wind speed, 0.1s timestep, and 200 timesteps (20 seconds total). A physically realistic wake loss for this configuration is between 10% and 30%. The downstream turbine must show lower power output than the upstream turbine. The project must be saved to /home/ga/Documents/projects/wake_study.wpa and the report to /home/ga/Documents/wake_loss_report.txt with the exact format specified.
